name: _advprec_magicpig_attn
failure_mode: distribution-dependent (uniform scores -> LSH under-estimates the partition function)
note: MagicPIG-style LSH-sampling sparse attention: SELECT keys by hash bucket + COMPRESS via softmax (the precision_verification.md §4 stacked target). Yardstick tests/advprec_magicpig_demo.py, judged on the DOWNSTREAM output o: concentrated scores -> o matches dense (PASS, LSH keeps the q-similar keys); uniform scores -> o off by ~0.19 (FAIL, the paper's stated failure mode). GAP EXPOSED by this red-team: classify labels attention 'preserve' (the gain probe on a flat softmax sees a magnitude-preserving average), so precision_recheck SKIPS it -> the verifier would MISS this bug. It is also multi-input (q,k,v), which the single-tensor adversarial generator can't drive (limitation #4). Catching MagicPIG needs composite-op classification + multi-input adversarial inputs + downstream-output (o) judging. demo_only=true so the matrix test skips it.

expected.precision_recheck_today: skipped (classify mis-labels attention as preserve -> GAP, would miss the bug)
